entities.name: vākya
entities.cite: while the sentence is being manifested by the causes of its manifestation

entities.name: buddhi
entities.cite: cognition first proceeds in the form of a seizing of parts

entities.name: bhāgāvagraha
entities.cite: the hearer's buddhi runs ahead in bhaga-avagraha-rupa, the form of part-seizure

entities.name: sphota
entities.cite: when the vakya-sphota is seized whole

entities.name: bhāga
entities.cite: it takes words, phrases, apparent constituents, as so many determinate grasps

axioms.name: pravartate
axioms.cite: the hearer's buddhi runs ahead in bhaga-avagraha-rupa, the form of part-seizure

axioms.name: anyatha
axioms.cite: These part-graspings are the anyatha-determinations of 1.89: committed, useful, and destined to be superseded when the vakya-sphota is seized whole

axioms.name: vivartate
axioms.cite: destined to be superseded when the vakya-sphota is seized whole

denials.name: nitya
denials.cite: destined to be superseded when the vakya-sphota is seized whole

claims.name: pravartate
claims.cite: cognition first proceeds in the form of a seizing of parts

claims.name: vivartate
claims.cite: cognition first proceeds in the form of a seizing of parts

rejected_readings.label: direct_whole_grasp
rejected_readings.rendering: Cognition directly grasps the undivided whole meaning without intermediate part-seizure.
rejected_readings.why: Bypasses the necessary intermediate stage of bhāgāvagraha (part-seizure) that the commentary emphasizes. This reverses the directional flow and violates the explicit sequence of the verse.

rejected_readings.label: permanent_parts
rejected_readings.rendering: The part-seizures constitute the final and permanent grasp of meaning.
rejected_readings.why: Directly contradicts the commentary's explicit statement that part-graspings are 'destined to be superseded when the vakya-sphota is seized whole'. Part-grasps are provisional scaffolding, not endpoint.

rejected_readings.label: sentence_as_final
rejected_readings.rendering: The sentence (vākya) is the final object of comprehension rather than the sphota.
rejected_readings.why: Confuses the manifestation (vākya) with its ontological ground. The commentary establishes that part-grasps are transcended by sphota seizure, making sphota the true endpoint.

notes: Verse 1.90 encodes the phenomenology of sentential comprehension: part-seizure (bhāgāvagraha) is the cognitive mode through which the hearer apprehends a sentence, but this mode is provisional and destined to be transcended by whole apprehension of the sphota. The theory describes hearing as a controlled progression through determinate grasps toward undivided meaning.
